response:
# Cheaper Alternatives to PostHog

## **Free/Open-Source Options**

| Tool | Best For | Cost |
|------|----------|------|
| **Plausible Analytics** | Privacy-focused web analytics | $9-20/month |
| **Fathom Analytics** | Simple site analytics | $14-74/month |
| **Matomo (Self-hosted)** | Full control over data | Free (self-hosted) |
| **Open Web Analytics** | Lightweight self-hosted | Free |
| **Mixpanel (Free tier)** | Event tracking | Free up to 1K users |

## **Budget-Friendly Paid Tools**

| Tool | Key Features | Starting Price |
|------|--------------|-----------------|
| **Amplitude** | Product analytics | Free tier available |
| **LogRocket** | Session replay + analytics | $99+/month |
| **Heap Analytics** | Auto-capture events | $99+/month |
| **Segment** | Data routing | Free tier available |
| **Simple Analytics** | Privacy-first | $19+/month |

## **Self-Hosted Alternatives**

- **Umami** - Lightweight, GDPR-compliant
- **GoAccess** - Real-time log analysis
- **Piwik PRO** - Enterprise Matomo alternative
- **Tracker.js** - Minimal JavaScript analytics

## **When to Consider Each**

- **Budget-conscious startups**: Plausible, Fathom, or Umami
- **Need event tracking**: Mixpanel free tier, Amplitude
- **Privacy-critical**: Plausible, Simple Analytics
- **Full control needed**: Matomo self-hosted, Umami

**Tip**: Many tools offer free tiers—test before committing. PostHog's main advantage is its all-in-one approach, so consider whether you truly need all its features.
